title: QUIC Hosted Interop-Runner Supported-Subset Coverage Work Item
summary: Add an opt-in manual GitHub Actions coverage profile that runs the existing interop helper across selected already-supported external-runner cells and uploads distinct artifact bundles.
planned_changes:
- Add a `workflow_dispatch` choice input that defaults to the existing `hosted-handshake` behavior and exposes an explicit `supported-subset` coverage profile.
- Keep the workflow outside push, pull-request, build, test, package, and support-readiness gates.
- Preserve the existing server-role handshake cell and add supported-subset matrix cells for a bounded same-slot retry cell, split-role transfer cells, and a client-role multiconnect cell using the helper's existing `handshakeloss` runner mapping.
- Write each attempted cell to a cell-specific artifact root and upload that tree with `if: always()`.
- Document the coverage profiles and add requirement-home tests that verify the workflow shape.
out_of_scope: making interop corroboration blocking in ordinary CI
verification_plan: Run the focused interop helper/script tests, run dry-run helper plans for the default handshake and supported-subset shapes, run SpecTrace core validation, and run Workbench core validation. Hosted `supported-subset` dispatch remains an external advisory artifact collection step rather than a prerequisite for this repository configuration slice.
completion_notes: Implemented an opt-in `supported-subset` coverage profile in `.github/workflows/interop-runner-handshake.yml`, preserved the default one-cell hosted handshake profile, documented both profiles in `scripts/interop/README.md`, and added requirement-home tests under `REQ-QUIC-INT-0017` to lock the manual/advisory workflow shape. Hosted supported-subset run 25187885762 on 2026-04-30 at commit 512a97764658bd3edab7a5b4113e72fcda908a0d completed successfully with uploaded runner reports for handshake, client transfer, server transfer, both-role retry, and client-role multiconnect/handshakeloss all marked `succeeded`; the result remains advisory evidence rather than a support-readiness gate.
